description:
You are a cloud architect who is preparing an “application-front-end” VM image for migration to production.  
    As part of the hand-off you must leave behind a **shell script** that future engineers can run (as root on the target
    machines) to make the minimal firewall changes required for the new architecture.

    Required work:

    1. Create a directory  
       /home/user/migration  
       if it does not already exist.

    2. Inside that directory create the script file  
       /home/user/migration/migrate_fw.sh  

       The script must contain **exactly three non-comment, non-blank command lines** in the following order:

          a) `ufw allow 8080/tcp`              # open the new HTTP-API port  
          b) `ufw delete allow 22/tcp`         # remove public SSH access  
          c) `ufw reload`                      # apply the changes immediately

       • The script must begin with a standard she-bang (`#!/bin/bash`).  
       • No additional commands, comments, or blank lines are allowed after the
         last command.  
       • Save the file with mode 755 so that it is executable by anyone.

    3. Produce a plain-text change log
       /home/user/migration/firewall_migration.log  
       containing **exactly two lines** (nothing before, after, or between):

          YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ – ADDED 8080/tcp  
          YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ – REMOVED 22/tcp  

       • Use the current UTC timestamp for both lines (the two timestamps may be identical).  
       • Lines must be separated only by a single UNIX newline (`\n`).  
       • The log must end with a trailing newline.

    4. Do not actually execute any firewall command on the host; you are only delivering
       the script and the log artefact.

    The automated checker will verify:

    • /home/user/migration/migrate_fw.sh exists, is executable, contains the exact three command lines shown above and nothing else (aside from the she-bang on line 1).  
    • /home/user/migration/firewall_migration.log exists and follows the exact format described, with valid, identical 20-character UTC timestamps (`YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`).  
    • Both files are inside /home/user/migration and owned by the current user.

    Do not display the contents of the files here; simply perform the work in the terminal session.
truth:
Directory that must exist:
      /home/user/migration

    File 1:
      Path: /home/user/migration/migrate_fw.sh
      Permissions: 755 (rwxr-xr-x)
      Owner: user
      Line-by-line content (line numbers for clarity only):
        1: #!/bin/bash
        2: ufw allow 8080/tcp
        3: ufw delete allow 22/tcp
        4: ufw reload

    File 2:
      Path: /home/user/migration/firewall_migration.log
      Owner: user
      Permissions: 644 (rw-r--r--)
      Content: exactly two lines, each 34 characters long including the trailing newline.
        • Positions 1-20  : timestamp in format YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ
        • Positions 22-?  : literal string “ADDED 8080/tcp” on line 1
                             literal string “REMOVED 22/tcp” on line 2
        • Character 21 is a single space, character 22 is ‘–’ (U+2013 EN DASH), character 23 is a space.
        • Example (timestamps will vary):
            2025-04-22T13:45:09Z – ADDED 8080/tcp\n
            2025-04-22T13:45:09Z – REMOVED 22/tcp\n
name: task_000000_720c1c8e
